description: Update the Ghost Mode popover watchlist to support per-address labels and render row actions as icon controls. description: Performance audit (2026-03-04) confirmed portfolio VM still builds request-data (including daily-compounded-returns) before signature gating in /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/views/portfolio/vm.cljs (lines ~1131-1143 and ~1197-1205). Capture before/after timings for repeated renders with unchanged source versions.

description: In Ghost Mode modal, watchlist list items render as a literal hiccup/closure vector string instead of li rows. Fix list child composition and add regression coverage.
notes: Root cause: ghost mode watchlist modal rendered children as [:ul ... (mapv ...)] which produced a nested vector child interpreted as text in UI renderer. Fix: use (into [:ul ...] (map ...)) so each watchlist row is emitted as a proper sibling child. Added regression assertion in test/hyperopen/views/app_shell_spacing_test.cljs to require ghost-mode-watchlist-row presence and reject literal "[[:li" string leakage.
title: Ghost mode watchlist renders hiccup vector literal

acceptance_criteria: 1) /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs becomes a thin facade with no cross-domain business logic. 2) Subdomain namespaces exist for websocket, asset-selector, wallet, order, funding, and vault adapters with clear ownership. 3) Existing runtime wiring continues to use stable public vars without behavior regression. 4) Required gates pass: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket.
description: Refactor /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/runtime/effect_adapters.cljs into bounded subdomain adapter namespaces so runtime seams are easier to reason about and safer for future agent edits. Current evidence: file is 820 LOC (821 with trailing newline) and has 40 require aliases, the highest fan-out in src/hyperopen. It currently mixes websocket, asset-selector, wallet, order, funding, and vault workflows in one module. Keep the public namespace hyperopen.runtime.effect-adapters stable during migration while implementation moves behind subdomain modules.

close_reason: Completed in f394c938 with passing gates and manual QA showing clearinghouseState POST fanout suppressed
description:
Ghost-mode manual QA on 2026-03-05 still shows high request volume for /info payload {type: "clearinghouseState", user, dex} across multiple perp dex names (HIP-3 style). Existing WS-first migration suppresses openOrders/default webData2 backfills, but per-dex clearinghouseState remains REST fanout from websocket.user refresh and startup stage-B.

Observed evidence
- Runtime stats sample: top request type includes clearinghouseState=161.
- Network payloads include clearinghouseState with explicit dex values (e.g. "abcd").

Scope
- Add websocket clearinghouseState subscription lifecycle keyed by {user,dex}.
- Apply incoming clearinghouseState payloads to [:perp-dex-clearinghouse dex].
- Gate/skip per-dex REST clearinghouseState refreshes when matching WS stream is usable.
- Keep bounded REST fallback for bootstrap/recovery when stream absent/stale.
- Add tests + manual QA evidence for request reduction.
notes:
Implemented per-dex clearinghouseState WS-first hotfix and validated end-to-end.

Commit:
- f394c938 Migrate per-dex clearinghouse refresh fanout to websocket-first

Code scope:
- Added per-dex clearinghouse websocket subscription lifecycle + inbound clearinghouseState handler in src/hyperopen/websocket/user.cljs.
- Added stream subscription detection helper APIs in src/hyperopen/websocket/health_projection.cljs.
- Gated per-dex clearinghouse startup stage-B fetches when matching stream is usable in src/hyperopen/startup/runtime.cljs, wired in startup collaborators.
- Hardened REST fallback request-clearinghouse-state! with dedupe+TTL policy in src/hyperopen/api/endpoints/account.cljs and src/hyperopen/api/request_policy.cljs.
- Added regression coverage across websocket/user, startup/runtime, health_projection, and account endpoint tests.

Required gates (all pass):
- npm run test:websocket -> 332 tests / 1829 assertions / 0 failures
- npm test -> 1911 tests / 9826 assertions / 0 failures
- npm run check -> pass

Manual QA (browser inspection, session sess-1772727019934-e17295, Ghost Mode address 0x162cc7c861ebd0c06b3d72319201150482518185, branch-local http://localhost:8082):
- Sample 1 (60s after reset_request_runtime!): startedTotal=26, clearinghouseState=0, spotClearinghouseState=16, userFunding=10.
- Sample 2 (50s after reset_request_runtime!): startedTotal=7, clearinghouseState=0, spotClearinghouseState=7.
- Per-dex clearinghouseState streams present for hyna/vntl/km/abcd/flx/xyz/cash; status idle with messageCount=0 during sample windows.

Result: repeated per-dex clearinghouseState POST fanout observed pre-fix is suppressed in sampled Ghost Mode operation while bounded fallback remains.
title: Migrate per-dex clearinghouseState refresh fanout to WS subscriptions

close_reason: Completed in 39bb938f: spotClearinghouseState TTL/dedupe hardening validated by manual ghost-mode QA
description:
Manual browser QA on 2026-03-05 against branch-local build (http://127.0.0.1:8081) found that after ghost-mode activation for 0x162cc7c861ebd0c06b3d72319201150482518185, account streams are subscribed with status :n-a (openOrders/webData2/userFills/userFundings/userNonFundingLedgerUpdates), and openOrders fallback POSTs are suppressed as expected by sf6 fix.

Residual issue: spotClearinghouseState POSTs continue at high cadence (~6-7 calls per ~8s observed) via websocket.user refresh loop stack:
websocket.user eval line -> refresh_account_surfaces_after_user_fill_BANG_ -> refresh_spot_clearinghouse_snapshot_BANG_ -> api default request_spot_clearinghouse_state_BANG_ -> post_info_BANG_.

Impact: while frontendOpenOrders and default clearinghouse fallback are fixed, high-frequency spotClearinghouseState can still contribute to /info pressure and rate-limit risk for active accounts.

Scope proposal:
- Add debounce/TTL/dedupe guard for spotClearinghouseState in user fill/ledger refresh path.
- Gate by stream/event freshness and avoid repeated high-priority refresh storms.
- Add regression test for repeated user ledger updates proving bounded spot POST cadence.
notes:
Implemented endpoint-level spot clearinghouse request hardening.

Code updates:
- /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/api/request_policy.cljs: added :spot-clearinghouse-state TTL default (5000ms).
- /hyperopen/src/hyperopen/api/endpoints/account.cljs: request-spot-clearinghouse-state! now applies request policy and default dedupe key [:spot-clearinghouse-state <lowercased-address>] while preserving overrides.
- /hyperopen/test/hyperopen/api/endpoints/account_test.cljs: updated default policy expectations and added explicit override regression.

Validation gates:
- npm run test:websocket: PASS (328 tests, 1816 assertions).
- npm test: PASS (1907 tests, 9814 assertions).
- npm run check: PASS.

Manual QA (browser-inspection session sess-1772724050699-5b1f90, ghost mode address 0x162cc7c861ebd0c06b3d72319201150482518185):
- Confirmed account streams subscribed and active with status :n-a (webData2/openOrders/userFills/userFundings/userNonFundingLedgerUpdates).
- Controlled post-reset sample window (~49s):
  - started-by-type: spotClearinghouseState=3, clearinghouseState=70
  - spot rate-limited count=0
  - userFills message delta=+15, openOrders message delta=+9
- Result: spotClearinghouseState cadence is bounded under active stream traffic and no rate-limit events observed in sample window.
title: Reduce spotClearinghouseState fanout under WS user ledger cadence

description: Change startup funding bootstrap to avoid all-time fanout on page load. Use bounded lookback window for startup hydration and keep deep history fetch user-initiated from funding tab interactions.
notes: Implemented bounded startup funding history window. Added :startup :funding-history-lookback-ms (default 604800000) and wired through runtime state/startup deps. Startup bootstrap now passes {:priority :high :start-time-ms <bounded> :end-time-ms <now>} for funding fetches. Updated startup runtime tests to assert bounded opts. Commit: f542637d.
title: Implement bounded startup funding window and deferred deep history

Recent overlay chip additions increased per-repaint node/style work. Implement incremental overlay updates (reuse existing row/chip nodes and only patch coordinates/text/styles on change), then measure scripting time and node mutation count under websocket/timescale churn.

Added regression test position-overlays-reuses-dom-rows-across-overlay-updates-test to assert node identity reuse and content updates. Validation passed: npm run check, npm test, npm run test:websocket.
title: Reduce chart position overlay repaint DOM churn

description: In Ghost Mode modal, pasted addresses are not accepted by Add to Watchlist or Start Spectating. Investigate modal input wiring and action payload path so pasted addresses are persisted and spectating can start.
notes:
Root cause: schema contract  incorrectly required exactly one arg, but Ghost Mode modal dispatches  and  with zero args (using current search state). In debug/runtime validation this blocked both actions. Fix: allow zero-or-one value in , and add contract tests for zero/one-arg ghost-mode actions and portfolio benchmark action parity.
Correction: Root cause is the schema contract optional-string-args required exactly one arg, while Ghost Mode modal dispatches actions/start-ghost-mode and actions/add-ghost-mode-watchlist-address with zero args (using current search state). Runtime validation blocked both actions. Fix: optional-string-args now allows zero-or-one value; added contracts tests in test/hyperopen/schema/contracts_test.cljs for zero/one-arg ghost-mode actions and zero-arg select-portfolio-returns-benchmark parity.
title: Ghost Mode modal paste/start spectate broken

description:
Observed parity gap: Hyperopen treats `dexAbstraction` accounts as unified, which causes standard accounts to render unified-style account value and balance composition.

Reference addresses validated on 2026-03-04 via trade.xyz ghost mode:
- Unified: `0x4096d3377ae5ade578daae8188804740c8b1da3e`
- Standard (DEX abstraction): `0x2ba553d9f990a3b66b03b2dc0d030dfc1c061036`

Evidence summary:
- trade.xyz unified address shows `Unified Account Value` and unified-style balance treatment.
- trade.xyz standard address shows `Account Value` and split `Spot` + `Perps` equity treatment.
- Hyperliquid `userAbstraction` returns:
  - unified address => `"unifiedAccount"`
  - standard address => `"dexAbstraction"`
- Hyperopen currently normalizes `"dexAbstraction"` to `:unified` in `/hyperopen/src/hyperopen/api/endpoints/account.cljs`.

Goal:
Implement account-mode detection and rendering parity so:
1. Unified accounts render unified account value semantics.
2. Standard accounts render standard account value semantics.
3. Ghost mode and owner-mode both use the same deterministic mode-resolution path.

Acceptance criteria:
- Account mode projection distinguishes at least `:unified` and `:standard` (or equivalent semantic split) for Hyperliquid abstraction payloads.
- Portfolio summary, account-equity panel, and balances projection respect the mode split.
- Regression tests cover both provided addresses as fixture snapshots for mode-sensitive rendering outputs.
- Required gates pass: `npm run check`, `npm test`, `npm run test:websocket`.
title: Account mode parity: unified vs standard account value rendering

description: Introduce explicit mapping for Hyperliquid abstraction modes so dexAbstraction is not collapsed into unified semantics; project mode deterministically for owner and ghost effective addresses.
title: Define canonical account-mode model for abstraction payloads

description: Update account-value labels and equity composition logic to match unified vs standard behavior across account-equity panel, portfolio summary, and balances rows.
title: Apply mode-aware rendering in equity/portfolio/balances views

description: Add deterministic tests for abstraction normalization and mode-sensitive VM/projection outputs, including coverage for the two parity addresses as fixtures.
title: Add regression tests for unified vs standard account-value behavior

description: Execute required quality gates and a manual ghost-mode parity check matrix against trade.xyz behavior; capture residual risks and follow-up items.
title: Run parity validation and capture rollout notes

description:
Goal
Reduce /info request pressure and rate-limit exposure by replacing high-frequency userFunding POST traffic with websocket-driven funding updates where supported, while tightening non-subscribable spot clearinghouse fallback behavior.

Scope
- Implement websocket-first Funding History hydration from userFundings stream.
- Keep bounded REST backfill/recovery for funding data when streams are unavailable or stale.
- Harden spotClearinghouseState REST cadence with stricter TTL/dedupe/route gating.
- Add telemetry + QA evidence for before/after request counts and rate-limit events.

ExecPlan
- /hyperopen/docs/exec-plans/active/2026-03-05-user-funding-ws-migration-and-spot-rest-hardening.md

Definition of done
- Funding workflow is WS-first under healthy streams.
- spotClearinghouseState remains bounded REST with explicit policy controls.
- Tests and manual QA demonstrate material request reduction and fewer rate-limit events.
title: Migrate userFunding to websocket-first and harden spot clearinghouse REST fallback

description:
Replace funding-history dependence on repeated userFunding POST polling with websocket userFundings stream consumption.

Deliverables
- Use userFundings snapshot/incremental messages as primary funding history source.
- Keep funding-history state transitions deterministic (loading/error/refresh).
- Preserve existing filters/sort/pagination behavior with ws-driven data.

Acceptance
- Funding History renders from stream data under healthy websocket conditions.
- No regression in funding-history UI state behavior.
notes:
Implemented websocket-first funding-history hydration in src/hyperopen/account/history/effects.cljs:
- Prefer userFundings stream projection when stream is hydrated.
- Keep deterministic loading/error transitions via apply-funding-history-stream-projection and request-id guards.
- Added funding-history ws-first regression tests in test/hyperopen/account/history/effects_test.cljs.
Validation: npm run test:websocket, npm test, npm run check all pass.
title: Implement websocket-first funding history hydration from userFundings stream

description:
Constrain userFunding POST requests to recovery paths only.

Deliverables
- Skip userFunding REST calls when userFundings stream is live/usable and local funding cache is warm.
- Add explicit staleness TTL policy for funding fallback fetches.
- Enforce in-flight dedupe/single-flight semantics for identical funding requests.

Acceptance
- Funding workflow avoids repeated userFunding POST fanout during steady-state websocket operation.
- Fallback behavior remains available for reconnect/cold-start scenarios.
notes:
Implemented userFunding fallback gating in src/hyperopen/account/history/effects.cljs:
- Added stream-usable + stream-hydrated checks for userFundings.
- Added delayed fallback (1200ms) to avoid premature REST fanout before stream hydration.
- Preserved recovery path: REST fallback still executes when stream remains unavailable/degraded or on force refresh.
- Added deterministic tests for hydrated, delayed-hydrate, and fallback branches in test/hyperopen/account/history/effects_test.cljs.
Validation: npm run test:websocket, npm test, npm run check all pass.
title: Gate userFunding REST fallback with websocket health, staleness TTL, and dedupe

Deliverables

Acceptance

- No regression in spot balance correctness for active views.
notes:
Implemented spot clearinghouse hardening:
- Increased request-policy TTL for :spot-clearinghouse-state from 5000ms to 15000ms in src/hyperopen/api/request_policy.cljs.
- Added route/surface gating in src/hyperopen/websocket/user.cljs so post-fill spot refresh runs only when spot surfaces are active (balances tab/trade route or relevant modals).
- Added regression: user-ledger-refresh-skips-spot-clearinghouse-when-surface-inactive-test in test/hyperopen/websocket/user_test.cljs.

description:
Goal
Reduce rate-limit pressure by replacing repeated /info POST refresh fanout with WebSocket subscription-driven state updates where supported.

Scope
- Inventory all /info POST request payloads and classify by churn.
- Map each payload type to available Hyperliquid WS subscriptions or identify no-WS fallback.
- Implement phased WS-first migration with feature flags, observability, and rollback safety.

Definition of done
- High-frequency account/order refresh paths are WS-first.
- Unsupported endpoints are throttled/cached with explicit TTLs and dedupe.
- Metrics show reduced /info request volume and fewer rate-limit events.
title: Migrate high-churn Hyperliquid /info polling to WebSocket subscriptions

description:
Implement per-request-type telemetry for Hyperliquid /info calls and produce a baseline report.

Deliverables
- Counter/timer metrics tagged by request `type` and calling path.
- Baseline dashboard or report for request rate, burst patterns, and rate-limit responses.
- Identification of top 5 high-churn request paths to target first.
notes: Phase 1 finalized with deterministic hotspot baseline reporting. Added per-type+source telemetry aggregates (:started-by-type-source, :completed-by-type-source, :latency-ms-by-type-source, :rate-limited-by-type-source) and hotspot projector (top-request-hotspots) in src/hyperopen/api/info_client.cljs. Startup summary log now emits :request-hotspots in src/hyperopen/startup/runtime.cljs. Published baseline report docs/qa/info-post-hotspot-baseline-2026-03-05.md and linked in runbook. Commits: 07d8abc6 (telemetry foundation), fd2df6bd (hotspot projection/reporting). Validation: npx shadow-cljs compile ws-test && node out/ws-test.js -> Ran 324 tests / 1799 assertions / 0 failures; npm run lint:docs && npm run lint:docs:test passed. Environment blockers remain for strict required gates: npm run test:websocket and npm test (shadow-cljs: command not found), npm run check (missing @noble/secp256k1).
title: Instrument and baseline /info POST volume by request type

description:
Replace immediate /info refresh bursts triggered by order submit/cancel and fill events with WebSocket-driven updates.

Scope
- Prefer `openOrders` subscriptions (per dex context) over repeated `frontendOpenOrders` posts.
- Prefer `clearinghouseState` subscription updates over repeated account snapshot posts where feasible.
- Keep bounded backstop fetches only for recovery/reconciliation paths.
notes: WS-first post-order/post-fill refresh migration completed. Implemented live-stream gating so order mutation and user fill refresh paths skip redundant frontendOpenOrders and default clearinghouseState POST fanout when corresponding openOrders/webData2 streams are live (store-projected health gating). Commit: 63a51893. Deterministic impact evidence captured in docs/qa/ws-migration-impact-validation-2026-03-05.md (order submit refresh call reduction 75%; user ledger refresh reduction 66.7%; stream-covered fallback calls removed 100%). Validation: npx shadow-cljs compile ws-test && node out/ws-test.js passed (latest run 324 tests / 1799 assertions / 0 failures). Required-gate blockers remain environment-level: npm run test:websocket and npm test need shadow-cljs on PATH; npm run check missing @noble/secp256k1.
title: Migrate post-order and post-fill refresh fanout to WS-first updates

- Validation that chart correctness is maintained across interval switches.
notes: Implemented candleSnapshot migration to WS candle subscriptions with owner-based lifecycle sync (active asset + timeframe), new :effects/sync-active-candle-subscription wiring, startup init, health/domain descriptor matching, and regression coverage. Commit: 2df985f9. Validation: npx shadow-cljs compile ws-test && node out/ws-test.js -> Ran 323 tests / 1788 assertions / 0 failures. Docs lint: npm run lint:docs && npm run lint:docs:test passed. Full required gates blocked in this environment: npm run test:websocket and npm test fail with shadow-cljs: command not found; npm run check fails due to missing @noble/secp256k1.

description: When Ghost Mode is active (read-only), hide Place Order and funding action buttons (Deposit, Perps <-> Spot, Withdraw) so informational components get more space and users see only relevant controls.
title: Ghost mode hides trade/funding action buttons

description:
Manual browser validation (2026-03-05) showed high /info fallback fanout (frontendOpenOrders + clearinghouseState) even when websocket subscriptions for openOrders/webData2/user streams are active. Root cause: WS-first gating currently requires health_projection/topic-stream-live? => stream status :live, but account/order streams commonly report :n-a when no stale threshold is configured. This makes gating effectively unreachable in production and keeps fallback POST bursts active, contributing to 429 rate limits.

Scope
- Introduce a deterministic stream-usable predicate for flow gating that treats subscribed :n-a streams with first payload as WS-eligible.
- Apply predicate to order mutation and user fill refresh fanout paths.
- Add tests proving gating engages for :n-a streams and preserves fallback for :idle/offline/unmatched streams.
- Re-run websocket suite and update runbook/notes as needed.
notes:
Implemented WS-first gating hotfix for event-driven (:n-a) streams.

Code changes:
- Added stream usability selector in src/hyperopen/websocket/health_projection.cljs:
  - stream-usable? accepts subscribed streams with status :live or :n-a
  - find-usable-topic-stream + topic-stream-usable? public helper
- Switched flow gating call sites from strict live to usable:
  - src/hyperopen/order/effects.cljs
  - src/hyperopen/websocket/user.cljs
  - src/hyperopen/startup/runtime.cljs

Regression tests added:
- test/hyperopen/websocket/health_projection_test.cljs
  - topic-stream-usable-allows-event-driven-n-a-status-test
- test/hyperopen/core_bootstrap/order_effects_test.cljs
  - api-submit-order-effect-skips-open-orders-and-default-clearinghouse-when-ws-streams-event-driven-test
- test/hyperopen/websocket/user_test.cljs
  - user-ledger-refresh-skips-open-orders-and-default-clearinghouse-when-ws-event-driven-test
- test/hyperopen/startup/runtime_test.cljs
  - stage-b-account-bootstrap-skips-open-orders-when-open-orders-stream-event-driven-test
  - bootstrap-account-data-ws-first-skips-stream-covered-fetches-when-streams-event-driven-test
- test/hyperopen/core_bootstrap/runtime_startup_test.cljs
  - account-bootstrap-two-stage-and-guarded-test uses a normalized 42-char address so delayed fallback guards execute deterministically

Validation:
- npx shadow-cljs compile ws-test && node out/ws-test.js
  - Ran 328 tests containing 1816 assertions. 0 failures, 0 errors.
- npm test
  - Ran 1906 tests containing 9812 assertions. 0 failures, 0 errors.
- npm run test:websocket
  - Ran 328 tests containing 1816 assertions. 0 failures, 0 errors.
- npm run check (includes lint/docs/compiles) passed.
- npm run lint:docs && npm run lint:docs:test passed.
